blocks.text: DTC P2138: Accelerator pedal position Sensor 1/2 correlation error

Accelerator pedal position sensor 1 and accelerator pedal position sensor 2 use Hall elements with different output voltage characteristics for each sensor, and are mounted on the accelerator pedal in the vehicle cabin. When the accelerator pedal is operated, the accelerator pedal position sensor is driven to detect the accelerator pedal opening. The accelerator pedal opening degree information signaled by the accelerator pedal position sensor is input to the PGM-FI ECU and used to calculate the target opening degree. Accelerator pedal position sensor 1 is for normal control, and accelerator pedal position sensor 2 serves as a backup in case accelerator pedal position sensor 1 fails. The output voltages are compared with each other for fault detection. If the variation of the accelerator pedal position sensor 2 output voltage continues outside the set range for more than the set time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: If one of the following conditions continues for 0.3 seconds or longer, or if 19 counts up in the next condition and the total count is 570 or more*.

items.text: When the accelerator pedal position sensor 1 voltage [AP sensor 1] is 0.37 V and the accelerator pedal position sensor 2 [AP sensor 2] voltage is 0 V or less or 0.37 V or more.
When the accelerator pedal position sensor 1 voltage [AP sensor 1] is 5.0 V and the accelerator pedal position sensor 2 voltage [AP sensor 2] is 2.31 V or less or 2.69 V or more.

blocks.text: *: Counts down by 1 except in the above states

items.text: Short circuit between PGM-FI ECU and accelerator pedal position sensor (APS1 line and APS2 line)

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
